title: Appendicitis
objectives: Identify key diagnostic criteria for Appendicitis
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Appendicitis** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Appendicitis. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Appendicitis, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Appendicitis.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Appendicitis
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: WSES Jerusalem Guidelines for Acute Appendicitis
Radiopaedia: CT Abdomen/Pelvis (or US in peds/pregnancy) Cases

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Appendicitis?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Appendicitis, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Appendicitis?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Appendicitis, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Appendicitis requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Appendicitis requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: CT with IV contrast is gold standard in adults; US first in children and pregnant patients
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Appendiceal diameter >6mm, wall enhancement, fat stranding, appendicolith
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Uncomplicated → surgery; perforation/abscess → may need drainage first; antibiotics alone in select cases
